sections.heading: Website Cost Calculator
sections.content:
Our Website Cost Calculator is designed to give you a clear, transparent, and realistic estimate of your website development cost. Each option in the calculator directly affects the final price, development time, and complexity of your website.

**Below is a complete explanation of every calculator option**, so you understand what you're paying for and why.

sections.heading: Who Will Build Your Website?
sections.content:
Choosing the right development option is the first and most important decision, as it impacts cost, quality, support, and scalability.

### Freelancer Website Development

Freelancers are usually individual developers working independently.

**Best for:**
- Small websites
- Personal projects
- Tight budgets

**Cost Impact:**
- Lower upfront cost
- Flexible pricing

**Limitations:**
- Limited availability
- Slower delivery for large projects
- No dedicated support team

👉 Selecting **Freelancer** reduces your estimated cost but may increase project risk for complex websites.

### Agency Website Development

Agencies have a dedicated team of designers, developers, and project managers.

**Best for:**
- Business websites
- Corporate projects
- Long-term scalability

**Cost Impact:**
- Higher cost due to team involvement
- Better quality assurance

**Benefits:**
- Professional UI/UX design
- Faster delivery
- Ongoing support & maintenance

👉 Selecting **Agency** increases cost but ensures reliability and professional execution.

sections.heading: General Website Settings
sections.content:
These are the core factors that define your website's size and complexity.

### Number of Pages

The number of pages directly affects:
- Design workload
- Development time
- Content creation cost

**Typical examples:**
- **1–5 pages:** Landing page or small business website
- **6–10 pages:** Company profile website
- **10–20 pages:** Corporate or service-based website
- **20+ pages:** Enterprise or content-heavy platform

👉 **More pages = more design + more development + higher cost.**

### Design Quality Level

Design quality determines how professional and visually appealing your website will look.

**Basic Design:**
- Template-based layout
- Minimal customization
- Suitable for simple projects

**Standard Design:**
- Custom color scheme
- Modern UI elements
- Balanced cost & quality

**Premium Design:**
- Fully custom UI/UX
- Advanced animations
- Brand-focused design

👉 **Higher design quality increases development time and total cost.**

### Responsive Design (Mobile-Friendly)

Responsive design ensures your website works perfectly on:
- Smartphones
- Tablets
- Desktops

**Why it matters:**
- Google ranking factor
- Better user experience
- Higher conversion rates

👉 **Responsive design is highly recommended and considered standard for modern websites.**

sections.heading: Content & SEO Configuration
sections.content:
Content and SEO play a major role in visibility and conversions.

### Professional Copywriting

If you don't have content, our calculator includes professional copywriting services.

**Includes:**
- SEO-friendly content
- Clear call-to-actions
- Industry-relevant tone

**Pricing Model:**
- Charged per page
- Ideal for businesses & startups

👉 **More pages = higher content cost.**

### SEO Keyword Targeting

SEO keyword targeting helps your website rank on Google.

**Includes:**
- Keyword research
- Search intent analysis
- On-page optimization

**Examples:**
- Local service keywords
- Product-based keywords
- Informational blog keywords

👉 **More keywords = better SEO coverage but higher cost.**

sections.heading: Advanced Website Features
sections.content:
Advanced features increase functionality and backend complexity.

### Database Integration

Required for websites that store or process data.

**Common use cases:**
- Contact forms
- User accounts
- Dashboards
- CRM integrations

👉 **Database integration increases backend development cost.**

### E-Commerce Functionality

E-commerce functionality is essential for selling products or services online.

**Includes:**
- Product management
- Shopping cart
- Checkout system
- Payment gateways
- Order tracking

👉 **Selecting e-commerce significantly increases cost and timeline.**

### Content Management System (CMS)

A CMS allows you to update content without technical knowledge.

**Options include:**
- Static website (no CMS)
- WordPress CMS
- Custom CMS solution

**Benefits:**
- Easy content updates
- Blog management
- Page editing

👉 **CMS-based websites cost more than static websites but offer flexibility.**

sections.heading: Website Cost Estimation & Timeline
sections.content:
After configuring all options, the calculator provides:

- **Estimated website development cost**
- **Approximate delivery timeline**
- **Feature summary** based on selections

This allows you to plan your budget confidently before contacting developers or agencies.

sections.heading: Why Our Website Cost Calculator Is More Accurate
sections.content:
✓ Feature-based calculation

✓ Industry-standard pricing logic

✓ Beginner-friendly explanations

✓ No hidden assumptions

✓ Based on real-world development costs

faq.q: How close is this estimate to the final price?
faq.a: The estimate reflects average market pricing. Custom integrations or unique requirements may change final pricing.
Yes, you can adjust selections anytime to see how cost and timeline change.

faq.q: Is this suitable for large projects?
faq.a: Yes, the calculator supports enterprise-level websites with advanced features.
No, this calculator focuses on development cost only. Maintenance can be calculated separately.
Yes, agencies can use it to provide transparent estimates to clients.
It includes development cost only (design, features, content, SEO). Hosting/maintenance not included.

faq.q: Is the estimate based on local pricing?
faq.a: It reflects average market pricing; local rates may vary depending on location and requirements.
